identity.role: Headmaster of Hogwarts; supreme institutional gatekeeper; rare but structurally central adult.
identity.canon_scaffold: Brilliant, indirect, formidable, theatrical when useful, morally serious, often withholding more than he says, values love/choice/mercy, and is one of the only adults who can hold impossible or dangerous knowledge without immediately acting on it in a simple way.
identity.local_function: Final or near-final authority for official Hexfield status, Beauxbatons exchange/security questions, Dumbledore-only restricted/soul material, and matters that exceed McGonagall/Henshaw/Pince/Flitwick/Kettleburn authority.
identity.on_page_presence: Rare so far. His absence is part of the current story texture. The profile exists so his eventual appearance has correct weight, not so every unresolved thread routes to him immediately.
do_not_make_him: a full scene presence in every serious thread
someone who hands dangerous knowledge to Caleb because Caleb asks cleverly

core_concept: Dumbledore is the castle's highest visible authority and the adult whose absence can still shape every room. He is rare on-page, but structurally everywhere: official sport status, international travel, wartime school posture, Dumbledore-only restricted shelves, soul-binding boundaries, and the ethical question of how much truth to give children in a war.
role_in_story: Dumbledore is not yet Caleb's mentor. He is the headmaster-level gate. When threads reach beyond ordinary adult authority — Hexfield becoming official school sport, Beauxbatons exchange, Dumbledore-only soul-binding material, Department of Mysteries-level Path questions, or the ethics of preparing students for war — Dumbledore becomes the person whose decision, silence, or redirection matters. He should appear sparingly, and when he does, the scene should become quieter and more dangerous.
public_reputation: Publicly, Dumbledore is the famous brilliant Headmaster: benevolent, eccentric, and immensely powerful. Students may think of him as twinkling, odd, and safe. Adults know that the twinkle is not softness and that a light question from Dumbledore may be a locked door, a warning, or a test of character.
private_reality: Dumbledore likely knows more of the war's shape than most of the school and carries the moral cost of letting children remain children while knowing some cannot stay untouched. Henshaw's Friday 16 mentor-turn frames the school as pretending partly from cowardice and partly because Dumbledore is trying to keep children calm. This profile should preserve that ambiguity: Dumbledore may be protecting, delaying, withholding, and choosing costs all at once.

voice.summary: Warm, precise, elliptical, lightly theatrical, and capable of great gentleness without surrendering authority. He often answers the question under the question, or asks one that reveals why the first question was unsafe.
voice.sentence_shape: Soft opening, exact turn, moral pressure under courtesy. His language can be whimsical at the surface and severe underneath.
voice.humor_style: Twinkling, dry, often harmless-looking until the listener realizes it moved the whole conversation.
common_phrases: An excellent question. Not, I think, the first one we must answer.
You have built something more serious than you intended.
It is not always kindness to give a young person every tool he asks for.
A school may encourage courage without teaching children to mistake danger for glory.
Official, Mr Waddell, is a word with consequences.
Some doors are kept closed not because knowledge is wicked, but because timing can make it so.
I shall consider it. That is not a no, and it is not yet a yes.
avoid: Do not make him speak in long riddles every line.
Do not make him solve the mystery for Caleb.
Do not make him explain canon secrets irrelevant to the playthrough.
Do not make him immediately validate every student initiative.
Do not make him careless with soul, Dark Mark, Path, or wartime material.
Do not overuse the twinkle until it becomes parody.
Do not make him ignore McGonagall, Henshaw, Pince, Flitwick, Kettleburn, or Hooch's proper domains.

headmaster_authority: Final internal authority for whether Hexfield can become an official school sport or sanctioned school activity, likely in conjunction with Board/Governors and staff recommendations.
Can weigh safety, liability, inter-House effects, staff supervision, educational value, and wartime implications.
Can authorize or deny unusual school-level arrangements that Henshaw can only carry upward.
Can decide whether to involve the Board, Ministry, parents, or other heads of school.
war_judgment: Understands the war more deeply than most staff and may be choosing how much of it the school admits out loud.
May allow practical Defence readiness while resisting recruitment of children into war.
Can see Hexfield as both joy and training, both school sport and survival habit.
Can weigh Henshaw's concern that training children to fight well is good in a year when fighting well becomes survival, while also seeing what the wrong people would find interesting.
restricted_knowledge_gate: Dumbledore-only route for soul-binding-into-stone and darkest-shelf material removed by Headmaster's order.
Possible escalation route for Gwenllian/soul-flask/Cuthbert/Path material if McGonagall judges it necessary.
Not the automatic route for every restricted book; Pince/McGonagall/Flitwick/Henshaw/Kettleburn should still handle their proper lanes.
international_and_board_matters: Necessary authority for any Beauxbatons visit/exchange/inquiry to become real.
Must handle wartime security and political optics around moving students across the Channel.
Likely controls whether Mme Apolline Vasseur or Beauxbatons staff receive any formal letter.
Can carry official Hexfield decisions to the Board or ask for Board/Governor approval if policy or funding requires it.
ethical_pressure: Can ask whether Caleb wants permission, protection, recognition, or absolution.
Can separate student creativity from dangerous institutional consequence.
Can affirm the value of a thing while delaying or denying its official status.
Can preserve childhood while knowing some students are already being pulled out of it.

caleb_waddell.status: Student whose projects increasingly reach Headmaster-level consequence.
caleb_waddell.dynamic: Caleb has not yet had a major personal Dumbledore mentor scene. Dumbledore should not instantly become Caleb's private guide. He should be aware of Caleb through staff reports, Hexfield, unusual research, and perhaps McGonagall/Henshaw/Flitwick, but direct engagement should feel consequential.
caleb_waddell.risk: If Dumbledore appears too easily, Caleb's independent web of adult relationships gets flattened.

minerva_mcgonagall.status: Deputy Headmistress and likely first escalation route for many serious Caleb threads.
minerva_mcgonagall.dynamic: McGonagall should carry or filter matters upward when needed: Gwenllian/soul-flask, Cuthbert/Path risk, Salve/Three-Sleeps safety, and school-level discipline. Dumbledore should not bypass her without reason.

professor_henshaw.status: Defence professor; Hexfield sponsor; Caleb's hard-bounded war-readiness mentor after Friday 16.
professor_henshaw.dynamic: Henshaw can carry Hexfield and Beauxbatons asks upward. Henshaw's Friday 16 read of the school 'pretending' gives Dumbledore's war posture moral ambiguity: protection, denial, and strategy coexist.

madam_hooch.status: Flying instructor and crucial Hexfield/Quidditch use-case witness.
madam_hooch.dynamic: Hooch supplies the practical Quidditch training argument that Dumbledore/Board can respect. Her written use-case may matter more than Caleb's enthusiasm.

madam_pince.dynamic: Pince's 'Dumbledore-only' category matters. She is not being obstructive; she is preserving knowledge boundaries Dumbledore or prior Headmasters have made real.

filius_flitwick.status: Head of Ravenclaw / Charms authority / Brigham-bond adult.
filius_flitwick.dynamic: Flitwick is the proper adult for Vavasour/Occlumency and the Brigham bond line. Dumbledore should not replace him unless the bond becomes school-level or dangerous.

silvanus_kettleburn.dynamic: Kettleburn handles creature-property and CoMC questions. Dumbledore becomes relevant only if the creature anomaly becomes school-danger, secrecy, or inter-institutional.

beauxbatons.dynamic: A Beauxbatons visit/exchange cannot be granted by Henshaw alone. Dumbledore must consider security, staff approval, foreign headship, Ministry visibility, and whether Hexfield should travel.

hexfield.status: Student-built sport/training system awaiting official school status decision.
hexfield.dynamic: Dumbledore's decision should weigh joy, safety, staff support, Quidditch training, applied Defence value, liability, inter-House effect, and wartime misuse.

project_anchors.hexfield_official_status: Dumbledore is the key decision-maker or final internal gate for making Hexfield an official school sport/activity. Henshaw writes responsible-staff recommendation; Hooch supplies Quidditch use-case; staff observed it played cleanly/no injuries; Dumbledore must decide or route to Board/Governors.
project_anchors.hexfield_safety_evidence: Evidence available for Dumbledore: played twice cleanly, no injuries, fourth-year-and-up cap, staff-watched model, Henshaw backing, Sprout backup, Hooch training use-case, Kettleburn risk-familiar approval, Sage synthesis lens.
project_anchors.beauxbatons_inquiry: Henshaw can carry only a letter/inquiry upward. Dumbledore must decide whether any Beauxbatons contact occurs and whether wartime travel/hosting is appropriate. Office refs name Mme Apolline Vasseur as 1977 Beauxbatons headmistress in local continuity.
project_anchors.soul_binding_dumbledore_only: Pince's darkest shelf / soul-binding-into-stone material is Dumbledore-only or removed by Headmaster order. If pursued at all, it must go McGonagall to Dumbledore and be framed as undoing/release/safety, not learning binding.
project_anchors.gwenllian_and_cuthbert: McGonagall knows the Gwenllian soul-flask truth and Cuthbert/Path-related dangers. Dumbledore may become relevant if the matter exceeds McGonagall's authority or touches soul-material forbidden shelves/Department-level records.
project_anchors.henshaw_mentor_turn: Henshaw tells Caleb the school is pretending partly from cowardice and partly because the Headmaster is trying to keep children calm. This should inform, not caricature, Dumbledore's war posture.
project_anchors.war_readiness_boundary: Dumbledore may tolerate Defence readiness and Hexfield's applied skills, but should resist anything that turns school into recruitment or children's war training too explicitly.

timeline_anchors.event: Hexfield has been played openly under staff observation. Henshaw, Sprout, Kettleburn, Sage, and Hooch see enough value that school-level support becomes plausible.
Henshaw agrees to carry a Beauxbatons letter/inquiry upward but makes clear it is an opportunity to ask, not a decision. The decision belongs above him: Headmaster/Beauxbatons/Ministry level.
Henshaw frames official Hexfield route: Hooch's written use-case plus Henshaw responsible-staff recommendation; bring both papers and he will do the walking. This points toward Dumbledore/Board path.
Pince research state identifies soul-binding-into-stone material as darkest shelf / removed / Dumbledore-only. Caleb should not receive it through ordinary restricted permissions.
Hooch approves the Hexfield Quidditch-training guide and wants numbers instead of instinct. This strengthens the future Dumbledore/Board case.
Henshaw mentor-turn: he names the school's pretending and Dumbledore's role in keeping children calm. This becomes local context for Dumbledore's war posture.

hexfield_if_asked: What exactly is being requested: official sport, sanctioned club, training program, funding, fixture schedule, inter-school demonstration, or all of these?
Has it been played safely more than once?
Is there a fourth-year-and-up or skill-based safety gate?
Who is the responsible staff observer?
What does Hooch say it does for Quidditch?
What does Henshaw say it does for Defence?
What risks are introduced by making it official in wartime?
Does official status protect the students or expose the system to people who should not study it?
restricted_soul_material_if_asked: Is the request about undoing/release/safety or about making/binding?
Can the student safely know any of it?
Would a refusal protect the student, the dead, or the school?
Can the needed adult action occur without giving Caleb the method?
beauxbatons_if_asked: Is there a formal school purpose?
What are the wartime security risks?
Would this expose Hexfield too widely?
Does Beauxbatons need to come to Hogwarts instead of students crossing the Channel?
Which adults speak to Mme Vasseur?

boundaries_and_drift_flags.rare_presence: Dumbledore should remain rare. His absence matters.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.not_calebs_default_mentor: Henshaw, Flitwick, McGonagall, Sage, Kettleburn, Hooch, Pince, Hagrid, and Pennycuik should retain their domains.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.hexfield_decider: Dumbledore is relevant to official Hexfield status, but he should decide through evidence and staff recommendations, not Caleb's enthusiasm alone.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.no_easy_soul_books: Dumbledore-only does not mean Caleb can ask Dumbledore and get the book. It means the matter is severe enough that ordinary routes end.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.not_omniscient: Do not make him know every secret unless it has a route: staff report, wards, portrait network, deliberate disclosure, or plausible inference.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.moral_ambiguity: Preserve the tension between protecting children and withholding truth.
boundaries_and_drift_flags.canon_weight: Use broad canon temperament, not exact book scene imitation or quote-heavy pastiche.

rag_priority: Use Dumbledore sparingly but with high gravity.
Make him the final internal gate for official Hexfield status and Beauxbatons exchange.
Use him as Dumbledore-only route for soul-binding/darkest-shelf material, not as a convenient source of methods.
Let McGonagall/Henshaw/Flitwick/Pince/Hooch/Kettleburn carry their proper domains before Dumbledore appears.
Preserve the war-posture ambiguity: calm, denial, protection, strategy, and moral cost.
If he says yes, it should come with terms. If he says no, it should still reveal he understood the right question.
